title: ajuga australis var. tridentata
intro: Ajuga australis var. tridentata, also known as the Australian bugle, is a variety of the Ajuga australis plant, a flowering plant native to Australia. It is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been found to help soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The plant's leaves and roots are commonly used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are consumed to take advantage of its medicinal properties, including reducing stress and promoting relaxation. In traditional Australian herbal medicine, Ajuga australis var. tridentata is also used to treat colds, coughs, and sore throats, and is believed to have antioxidant and immune-boosting properties.
